correctness.rationale: WHOOP and Garmin-activity rows correctly derive dates and ET/local projections with the −6h rule, but Tier-2 cycle-anchoring (use the active WHOOP cycle’s offset for other sources) is not implemented anywhere; most non-WHOOP sources fall straight to user_tz_log → ET. Provenance is also mis-assigned in some places (e.g., Garmin HRV). These divergences can produce wrong local/behavioral dates on travel days unless user_tz_log is perfectly maintained.

robustness.rationale: DST artifacts and transition days are explicitly handled (transition flag; tz_log_gaps distinguishes DST vs travel). NULL ts are guarded. GPS backfill skips same-offset noise. However, the missing cycle-anchor fallback reduces resilience when user_tz_log is incomplete or WHOOP is the only TZ signal.

scalability.rationale: All derivations are trigger-based with a single stable function. tz_for_instant is O(log n) with an index. LATERAL LIMIT 1 patterns avoid explosion. Backfills use no-op UPDATEs and a window-function pass for transitions. Overhead is acceptable for n=1 and would scale to 10x comfortably.

idiomaticness.rationale: Uses AT TIME ZONE correctly, BEFORE triggers, stable functions, and LATERAL joins. Minor nits (provenance handling via notes inspection; UTC+offset arithmetic) but overall clean, readable SQL and minimal bespoke mechanics.

response.summary: The implementation lands the three onyx_* dates and derives them consistently for sources with their own TZ signals (WHOOP timezone_offset; Garmin activities’ local−UTC delta). The −6h behavioral boundary is applied uniformly where an instant exists, and helpful operational features are in place (transition-day flag; tz_log_gaps with DST/travel discrimination). The main divergence from ADR-0001 is the absence of Tier-2 cycle anchoring for sources without a native TZ field (Spotify, Garmin sleep/HRV, journal/meal times): triggers fall back directly to user_tz_log → ET default, so non-WHOOP events on travel days are mis-attributed unless the log is comprehensive. Provenance is also leaky: per-row onyx_tz_source can’t distinguish whether tz resolution came from a GPS- or WHOOP-derived tz_log entry. Two smaller defects: garmin_hrv rows without a start_timestamp are labeled default_et_fallback (should be source_field or use cycle anchor), and whoop_tz_backfill has a NameError that prevents inserts. Top fixes: (1) implement cycle-anchor lookup in pds.derive_onyx_dates (or a sibling) and use it in triggers before tz_for_instant; (2) fix whoop_tz_backfill’s NameError and enforce resolution priority (Manual > GPS > WHOOP) deterministically; (3) carry provenance through user_tz_log (add a source enum) and surface it via tz_for_instant so onyx_tz_source is accurate. With these changes, the code will fully align with ADR-0001 and be robust on transition/travel days without relying on perfect manual logging.

findings.title: Tier-2 WHOOP cycle anchor not applied for non-WHOOP sources
findings.description: ADR-0001 specifies using the active WHOOP cycle’s timezone_offset for any event falling within that cycle’s window. The current derivation only uses (a) source-provided offset or (b) user_tz_log → ET fallback; no trigger consults the cycle to resolve TZ.
findings.evidence: Example triggers pass NULL tz_offset_text: spotify_plays (sql/adr_0001_04_other_onyx_dates.sql, set_onyx_dates_spotify_plays) and garmin_sleep (sql/adr_0001_03_garmin_onyx_dates.sql, set_onyx_dates_garmin_sleep) call derive_onyx_dates(ts, NULL, NULL), which resolves via tz_for_instant only.
findings.recommendation: Add a Tier-2 step: a function pds.cycle_offset_for_instant(ts) that returns wc.timezone_offset when ts ∈ [cycle.start_time, cycle.end_time), and modify derive_onyx_dates (or wrapper) to try cycle anchor before user_tz_log. Set onyx_tz_source='cycle_anchor' when used.

findings.title: whoop_tz_backfill NameError prevents any proposed inserts
findings.description: The notes string references an undefined variable log_offset_str, causing a runtime error and aborting proposals, so user_tz_log never gets WHOOP-based entries.
findings.evidence: notes=f"whoop-auto: cycle {…}; …; log was {log_tz} ({log_offset_str})" — variable log_offset_str is never defined.
findings.recommendation: Replace with log_offset_at_start (and/or end) in the f-string and add a quick unit test for infer_proposed_inserts so this can’t regress.

findings.title: Per-row provenance loses GPS/WHOOP origin once tz comes via user_tz_log
findings.description: When tz_offset_text is NULL, onyx_tz_source is set to 'user_tz_log' or 'default_et_fallback'. If tz_log was auto-populated from GPS or WHOOP, events do not reflect 'gps_inferred' or 'cycle_anchor' as specified by ADR-0001.
findings.evidence: ELSE branch in derive_onyx_dates assigns resolved_source := 'user_tz_log' or 'default_et_fallback'; no path to 'gps_inferred' or 'cycle_anchor' without a direct offset.
findings.recommendation: Add a provenance column to pds.user_tz_log (enum) and have tz_for_instant return (tz, source). Propagate that into onyx_tz_source so row-level provenance aligns with ADR.

findings.title: garmin_hrv fallback mislabels provenance as default_et_fallback
findings.description: When start_timestamp is NULL, all three onyx_* dates are set to calendar_date with onyx_tz_source='default_et_fallback'. That isn’t an ET fallback — it’s a source-provided clock date.
findings.evidence: ELSE branch assigns NEW.onyx_* := NEW.calendar_date and NEW.onyx_tz_source := 'default_et_fallback'.
findings.recommendation: Set onyx_tz_source='source_field' in this path (or apply cycle-anchor if available). Keep default_et_fallback only for true ET fallbacks.

findings.title: Cycle anchor also missing for Garmin sleep
findings.description: Behavioral-day attribution for garmin_sleep uses tz_for_instant directly. On travel days without a tz_log row, bedtime may be attributed to ET instead of the cycle’s offset.
findings.evidence: Trigger calls derive_onyx_dates(NEW.sleep_start, NULL, NULL) — no attempt to consult whoop_cycles for timezone_offset.
findings.recommendation: Use the new Tier-2 helper (see F-001) to resolve via the active WHOOP cycle before falling back to tz_for_instant.

findings.title: Supabase RPC call to schema-qualified function may not resolve
findings.description: supabase-py RPC often exposes only public schema functions. Using .schema('pds').rpc('tz_for_instant', ...) may fail unless PostgREST is configured to expose pds functions.
findings.recommendation: Create a SECURITY DEFINER wrapper in public schema (public.tz_for_instant_pds) or adjust RPC usage per Supabase docs to ensure calls succeed.

findings.title: Resolution priority Manual > GPS > WHOOP depends on run order
findings.description: Scripts skip inserts when tz_for_instant already resolves the instant, but neither enforces that GPS-derived entries take precedence over WHOOP if both run concurrently or out of documented order.
findings.evidence: Comments specify priority but no mechanism ensures GPS precedes WHOOP beyond operator discipline.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) persist a provenance field in user_tz_log and allow GPS to supersede same-offset WHOOP rows within a small window, or (b) provide a single orchestrator that runs WHOOP first, GPS second deterministically.

findings.title: Default-ET labeling leaks into date-only tables inconsistently
findings.description: For date-only tables, onyx_tz_source varies between 'source_field', 'cycle_anchor', and 'default_et_fallback'. Semantics should reflect whether the date is a deliberate behavioral date, not an ET fallback.
findings.evidence: whoop_journal sets onyx_et_date/local_date = cycle_date and toggles source based on behaviors_date presence; habit_journal labels 'default_et_fallback'.
findings.recommendation: Standardize: for date-only behavioral sources, set onyx_behavioral_date and onyx_tz_source='source_field' (or 'cycle_anchor' where explicitly derived), and leave et/local as null or equal to behavioral with 'source_field'.

findings.title: Null-ts path returns NULL provenance
findings.description: When ts is NULL, the function returns NULL for onyx_tz_source, which can complicate downstream filters expecting a known provenance bucket.
findings.evidence: IF ts IS NULL THEN RETURN ... NULL::TEXT;
findings.recommendation: Return onyx_tz_source='default_et_fallback' (or 'no_instant') for clarity; document the contract.

findings.title: daily_health_matrix_behavioral spine includes GDS calendar_date
findings.description: ADR describes the spine as the union of onyx_behavioral_date across sources; the view also unions garmin_daily_summary.calendar_date, which may add dates not present in any onyx_behavioral_date.
findings.recommendation: Either document this intentional inclusion or switch to onyx_behavioral_date in GDS once populated to keep semantics uniform.

things_done_well.title: Centralized derivation with idiomatic AT TIME ZONE and BEFORE triggers
things_done_well.why_it_matters: Keeps attribution logic consistent across sources and reduces drift; easy to harden once Tier-2 is added.

things_done_well.title: Garmin activities recover per-row offsets cleanly
things_done_well.why_it_matters: Leverages the local−UTC delta to avoid relying on external logs; aligns with ADR Tier-1.

things_done_well.title: Explicit transition-day flag with efficient backfill
things_done_well.why_it_matters: Surfaces ambiguity at TZ boundaries and lets downstream consumers filter/weight appropriately without O(N^2) trigger cascades.

things_done_well.title: DST-aware tz_log_gaps view distinguishes travel vs DST artifacts
things_done_well.why_it_matters: Avoids false positives on DST nights and provides actionable signals to maintain user_tz_log integrity.

things_done_well.title: Behavioral matrix view dedup logic for WHOOP cycles
things_done_well.why_it_matters: Picking the longest cycle and aggregating the transition flag yields stable daily rows on complicated nights.

things_done_well.title: GPS backfill’s same-offset guard prevents noisy IANA churn
things_done_well.why_it_matters: Skipping entries where offsets match avoids needless log entries (e.g., different city names with same EDT).

questions_for_followup: Should Tier-2 use [cycle.start_time, cycle.end_time) strictly, or window to nearest cycle when end_time is NULL or naps occur?
Do we want per-row provenance to reflect the origin of the tz_log entry (gps/WHOOP/manual)? If so, add a source enum on pds.user_tz_log and return it from tz_for_instant.
Confirm Supabase RPC exposure for schema-qualified functions; do we need a public wrapper for pds.tz_for_instant?
For garmin_hrv without start_timestamp, should we try cycle-anchor before falling back to calendar_date, or keep calendar_date with onyx_tz_source='source_field'?
Should we null onyx_et_date/onyx_local_date on date-only behavioral sources to avoid implying clock-date precision that doesn’t exist?
